name: Root
description: Lightweight orchestrator implementing PipelineProtocol that wires together all Exemplar sub-components into an end-to-end governed code review pipeline. Root accepts ExemplarConfig, injects protocol-typed dependencies (ClockProvider, IdGenerator, ChroniclerEmitter, SealChainStoreProtocol), and orchestrates the async pipeline: intake.run_intake → circuit.run → assessor.merge_assessments → reporter.format_report + seal_report. Root contains zero business logic — only wiring, sequencing, error handling at stage boundaries, and Chronicler event emission with PACT key attribution. Supports async context manager for resource lifecycle (opening/closing seal chain store, chronicler log). All data flowing through the pipeline uses frozen Pydantic v2 models from schemas. Root itself is NOT a frozen model — it holds mutable runtime references to component instances.

types.name: PipelineStage
types.description: Named stages of the Root orchestration pipeline, used for error boundary identification and Chronicler event context. StrEnum in implementation.

types.name: ErrorSeverity
types.description: Classification of pipeline errors by recoverability. Fatal errors abort the pipeline; recoverable errors produce partial results; ignorable errors are logged and suppressed.

types.name: ExemplarError

fields.name: stage
fields.description: The pipeline stage where the error occurred.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error description.

validators.error_message: Error message must not be empty

fields.name: pact_key
fields.description: PACT attribution key of the method that produced this error.

validators.error_message: PACT key must follow exemplar.root.method_name pattern

fields.name: cause
fields.description: String representation of the underlying exception that triggered this error, if any.

fields.name: error_severity
fields.description: Whether this error is fatal, recoverable, or ignorable.

fields.name: context
fields.description: Arbitrary key-value context for debugging (e.g., review_request_id, stage config).

types.description: Base exception for all Root-level pipeline errors. Carries the failing stage, a human-readable message, optional cause exception context, and PACT key for attribution. Subclass of Exception in implementation.

types.name: PipelineStageError

fields.name: stage
fields.description: The pipeline stage where the error occurred.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error description.

fields.name: pact_key
fields.description: PACT attribution key.

fields.name: cause
fields.description: String repr of underlying exception.

fields.name: error_severity
fields.description: Recoverability classification.

fields.name: context
fields.description: Debugging context.

fields.name: component_id
fields.description: The component_id of the sub-component that failed (e.g., 'intake', 'circuit', 'assessor', 'reporter').

types.description: Error specific to a pipeline stage failure, wrapping the underlying component error with stage context. Subclass of ExemplarError in implementation.

fields.name: ROOT_RUN_PIPELINE

fields.name: ROOT_BUILD_PIPELINE_RESULT
fields.description: PACT key for _build_pipeline_result. Value: 'exemplar.root.build_pipeline_result'

types.description: Module-level PACT key constants for all Root public methods. Used in Chronicler event payloads and ExemplarError attribution. All values are class-level str constants in implementation.

types.name: RootConfig

fields.name: chronicle_log_path
fields.description: File path for Chronicler JSON-lines log output.

validators.error_message: Chronicle log path must not be empty

fields.name: seal_chain_path
fields.description: File path for Tessera seal chain state.

validators.error_message: Seal chain path must not be empty

fields.description: File path for Kindex persistent store.

validators.error_message: Kindex store path must not be empty

types.description: Frozen Pydantic model extracting the Root-relevant subset of ExemplarConfig. Constructed by Root.__init__ from the full ExemplarConfig. Carries paths for chronicle log, seal chain, kindex store, and stigmergy store, plus the full sub-configs needed to construct components.

types.name: PipelineEvent

fields.name: event_type
fields.description: Well-known Chronicler event type string.

fields.name: stage
fields.description: Pipeline stage name, if applicable.

fields.name: message

types.description: A Chronicler event accumulated during pipeline execution. Lightweight wrapper to track events emitted at stage boundaries before they are included in PipelineResult.

types.name: ExitCodeMapping

fields.name: pass_code
fields.description: Exit code for pass decision. Value: 0.

fields.name: warn_code
fields.description: Exit code for warn decision. Value: 1.

types.description: Static mapping from ReviewDecision to integer exit codes. pass→0, warn→1, block→2. Used by Root to populate PipelineResult.exit_code.

functions.name: __init__
functions.description: Construct a Root orchestrator instance. Validates and slices ExemplarConfig into sub-configs for each component. Instantiates governance primitives (ChronicleEmitter, TesseraSealer, LedgerClassifier, KindexStore, StigmergyTracker). Constructs BatonCircuitRouter with reviewer instances from get_all_reviewers(). Stores injected protocol-typed dependencies (ClockProvider, ChroniclerEmitter, SealChainStoreProtocol). Does NOT open file handles or perform I/O — that happens in __aenter__. PACT key: exemplar.root.__init__.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: A valid ExemplarConfig instance. Root extracts sub-configs for circuit, reviewers, ledger, apprentice, and file paths.

inputs.name: clock
inputs.description: Injectable ClockProvider for deterministic testing. If None, a real-time clock is used.

inputs.name: chronicler
inputs.description: Injectable ChroniclerEmitter for event emission. If None, a file-based ChronicleEmitter is constructed from config.chronicle_log_path.

inputs.name: chain_store
inputs.description: Injectable SealChainStoreProtocol for Tessera seal chain. If None, a file-based store is constructed from config.seal_chain_path.

inputs.name: id_generator
inputs.description: Injectable callable returning UUID4 hex strings for deterministic testing. If None, uuid.uuid4().hex is used.

error_cases.name: invalid_config
error_cases.condition: config is not a valid ExemplarConfig or fails internal extraction

error_cases.name: circuit_config_error
error_cases.condition: CircuitConfig is invalid or reviewers cannot be registered (e.g., no enabled reviewers for a configured stage)

error_cases.name: invalid_clock_protocol
error_cases.condition: Provided clock does not satisfy ClockProvider protocol

error_data.detail: clock must implement ClockProvider protocol (now_utc, monotonic_ms)

error_cases.name: invalid_chronicler_protocol
error_cases.condition: Provided chronicler does not satisfy ChroniclerEmitter protocol

error_data.detail: chronicler must implement ChroniclerEmitter protocol (async emit)

preconditions: config is a valid ExemplarConfig that passes all Pydantic validators
If clock is provided, it satisfies ClockProvider protocol (now_utc() -> str, monotonic_ms() -> int)
If chronicler is provided, it satisfies ChroniclerEmitter protocol (async emit(event) -> None)
If chain_store is provided, it satisfies SealChainStoreProtocol (async get_previous_hash() -> str|None, async append_seal(seal) -> None)
postconditions: self._config holds the original ExemplarConfig reference
self._root_config is a RootConfig extracted from the ExemplarConfig
self._circuit_router is a fully constructed BatonCircuitRouter with all enabled reviewers registered
self._clock, self._chronicler, self._chain_store, self._id_generator are stored (injected or default-constructed)
self._is_open is False (no resources opened until __aenter__)
No file I/O has been performed

functions.name: __aenter__
functions.description: Async context manager entry. Opens file-based resources: ensures chronicle log directory exists, initializes seal chain store file if absent, initializes learner state directory. Returns self for use in 'async with' blocks. PACT key: exemplar.root.__aenter__.

error_cases.name: already_open
error_cases.condition: self._is_open is True (double entry into context manager)

error_data.message: Root context manager is already open

error_cases.name: filesystem_permission_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot create chronicle log directory, seal chain file, or learner state directory due to permissions

error_cases.name: learner_init_failure
error_cases.condition: learner.initialize_state() fails (corrupt existing state)

preconditions: Root was successfully constructed via __init__
self._is_open is False (not already entered)
postconditions: self._is_open is True
Chronicle log directory exists on filesystem
Seal chain store file is initialized (empty chain if new)
Learner state directory is initialized via learner.initialize_state()
Returned value is self

structured_side_effects.description: Ensures chronicle log directory exists via os.makedirs
Initializes seal chain store file if absent
Initializes .exemplar/learner/ directory and state.json/records.json if absent

functions.name: __aexit__
functions.description: Async context manager exit. Performs cleanup: flushes any buffered chronicle events, closes file handles. Suppresses no exceptions (re-raises all). PACT key: exemplar.root.__aexit__.

inputs.name: exc_type
inputs.description: Exception type if context exited with error.

inputs.name: exc_val
inputs.description: Exception value if context exited with error.

inputs.name: exc_tb
inputs.description: Exception traceback if context exited with error.

error_cases.name: cleanup_failure
error_cases.condition: File handle flush or close raises an I/O error during cleanup

error_data.message: Cleanup failure during context exit — logged and suppressed

preconditions: self._is_open is True
postconditions: self._is_open is False
All file handles have been closed or flushed
Return value is False (exceptions are not suppressed)

structured_side_effects.description: Flushes any buffered chronicle events to log file
Logs cleanup status and any errors during resource release

functions.name: run_pipeline
functions.description: Execute the full end-to-end review pipeline. Orchestrates: (1) emit review.started Chronicler event, (2) intake.run_intake to parse and classify diff, (3) circuit.run to route hunks through reviewer stages, (4) assessor.merge_assessments to combine assessments with trust-weighted scoring, (5) reporter.format_report to render output, (6) reporter.seal_report to apply Tessera seal, (7) emit review.complete Chronicler event. Error boundaries: intake/circuit failures with zero usable results are fatal (raise PipelineStageError); reviewer timeouts produce partial Assessments (recoverable); chronicler/seal failures are fire-and-forget (ignorable). Accumulates all Chronicler events emitted during the pipeline for inclusion in PipelineResult. Maps final ReviewDecision to exit_code (pass→0, warn→1, block→2). PACT key: exemplar.root.run_pipeline.

inputs.name: diff_source
inputs.description: Raw unified diff text to review. Must be non-empty.

validators.error_message: output_format must be one of: json, md, github

inputs.name: metadata
inputs.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata to attach to the ReviewRequest (PR number, branch, author, etc.).

error_cases.name: not_open
error_cases.condition: self._is_open is False — run_pipeline called outside async context manager

error_data.message: Root context manager must be entered before running pipeline

error_cases.name: empty_diff
error_cases.condition: diff_source is empty string

error_cases.name: intake_failure
error_cases.condition: intake.run_intake returns zero hunks and one or more fatal errors (completely unparseable diff)

error_cases.name: circuit_failure
error_cases.condition: circuit.run raises an unexpected error (not a reviewer timeout, which is handled internally)

error_cases.name: assessor_failure
error_cases.condition: assessor.merge_assessments raises ValueError (invalid input data)

error_cases.name: reporter_format_failure
error_cases.condition: reporter.format_report raises ReporterRenderError or ReporterFormatError

error_cases.name: seal_failure
error_cases.condition: reporter.seal_report raises ReporterSealError or ReporterChainStoreError — fire-and-forget, report is returned unsealed

error_cases.name: invalid_output_format
error_cases.condition: output_format is not one of 'json', 'md', 'github'

preconditions: self._is_open is True (Root context manager has been entered)
diff_source is a non-empty string containing unified diff text
output_format is a valid OutputFormat variant string
postconditions: Returned PipelineResult.review_request contains parsed and classified DiffHunks
Returned PipelineResult.assessments contains one Assessment per configured review stage
Returned PipelineResult.report is a merged ReviewReport with TesseraSeal attached (if sealing succeeded)
Returned PipelineResult.formatted_output contains the report rendered in the requested format
Returned PipelineResult.output_format matches the requested output_format
Returned PipelineResult.exit_code is 0 (pass), 1 (warn), or 2 (block)
Returned PipelineResult.events contains all Chronicler events emitted during pipeline execution
A review.started ChroniclerEvent was emitted at pipeline start (fire-and-forget)
A review.complete ChroniclerEvent was emitted at pipeline end (fire-and-forget)
All findings in the report are sorted deterministically per assessor contract

structured_side_effects.target: diff source (passed as string, but may originate from file)
structured_side_effects.description: Processes diff text through intake pipeline
Emits review.started, stage boundary, and review.complete Chronicler events
Appends Tessera seal to chain store via reporter.seal_report
Logs pipeline progress, errors, and fire-and-forget failures to stderr via stdlib logging

performance_budget.complexity: O(n * r) where n = number of diff hunks and r = number of review stages

functions.name: _run_intake_stage
functions.description: Internal method: executes the intake stage of the pipeline. Creates a string-based DiffSource from the raw diff text, invokes intake.run_intake with the LedgerConfig and ChroniclerEmitter, and returns the IntakeResult. Emits stage.started and stage.complete Chronicler events. PACT key: exemplar.root.run_pipeline (attributed to parent).

inputs.name: accumulated_events
inputs.description: Mutable list collecting ChroniclerEvents emitted during the pipeline.

error_cases.name: intake_read_failure
error_cases.condition: DiffSource.read() raises an IOError

error_cases.name: intake_parse_failure
error_cases.condition: parse_diff returns zero hunks with errors

preconditions: diff_text is a non-empty string
accumulated_events is an initialized (possibly empty) list
postconditions: Returned IntakeResult.review_request.hunks has zero or more classified DiffHunks
stage.started and stage.complete events have been appended to accumulated_events
ChroniclerEmitter emission failures are caught and logged (fire-and-forget)

structured_side_effects.description: Emits stage.started and stage.complete Chronicler events (fire-and-forget)

functions.name: _run_circuit_stage
functions.description: Internal method: executes the circuit stage. Invokes BatonCircuitRouter.run() with the ReviewRequest from intake. Returns the list of Assessments (one per configured stage). Reviewer timeouts and failures produce partial Assessments (handled by circuit internally). Emits stage boundary events. PACT key: exemplar.root.run_pipeline (attributed to parent).

inputs.name: review_request
inputs.description: A valid ReviewRequest with at least one DiffHunk.

error_cases.name: circuit_run_failure
error_cases.condition: BatonCircuitRouter.run() raises ValueError (invalid request)

preconditions: review_request.hunks is non-empty
BatonCircuitRouter was successfully constructed in __init__
postconditions: Returned list has exactly len(config.stages) Assessments
Assessments are ordered by their position in CircuitConfig.stages
Partial assessments (is_partial=True) are included for timed-out/failed reviewers

functions.name: _run_assess_stage
functions.description: Internal method: executes the assessment merge stage. Retrieves trust adjustments from learner (if phase is canary or primary), resolves trust scores, invokes assessor.merge_assessments. Returns the merged ReviewReport. Emits stage boundary events. PACT key: exemplar.root.run_pipeline (attributed to parent).

inputs.name: assessments
inputs.description: List of Assessment objects from circuit stage.

error_cases.name: assessor_merge_failure
error_cases.condition: assessor.merge_assessments raises ValueError

error_cases.name: learner_trust_failure
error_cases.condition: learner.get_trust_adjustments() fails — fire-and-forget, default trust weights used

error_data.message: Learner trust adjustment failed; using default weights

preconditions: assessments is a non-empty list of valid Assessment objects
All assessments have review_request_id matching review_request_id parameter
postconditions: Returned ReviewReport.review_request_id matches the input review_request_id
Returned ReviewReport.findings are sorted deterministically
Returned ReviewReport.decision is one of block, warn, pass
Trust adjustments from learner are applied if learner phase is canary or primary

structured_side_effects.description: Reads trust adjustments from .exemplar/learner/state.json if learner phase is canary/primary

functions.name: _run_format_and_seal_stage
functions.description: Internal method: executes the format and seal stages. Invokes reporter.format_report to render the ReviewReport, then reporter.seal_report to apply Tessera seal. Seal failure is fire-and-forget (report is returned unsealed). Emits stage boundary and report.sealed events. PACT key: exemplar.root.run_pipeline (attributed to parent).

error_cases.name: format_failure
error_cases.condition: reporter.format_report raises ReporterRenderError

error_cases.name: seal_failure
error_cases.condition: reporter.seal_report raises any error — fire-and-forget

preconditions: report is a valid ReviewReport instance
output_format is one of 'json', 'md', 'github'
postconditions: Returned dict has keys 'sealed_report' (ReviewReport, possibly with seal) and 'formatted_output' (FormattedReport)
If sealing succeeded, sealed_report.seal is a valid TesseraSeal
If sealing failed, sealed_report.seal is None and a warning is logged
formatted_output.content is a non-empty string in the requested format

structured_side_effects.description: Appends seal to chain store file via SealChainStoreProtocol
Emits stage boundary and report.sealed Chronicler events (fire-and-forget)

functions.name: _build_pipeline_result
functions.description: Internal pure helper: assembles a PipelineResult from the outputs of all pipeline stages. Maps ReviewDecision to exit_code (pass→0, warn→1, block→2). Pure function except for clock injection for timestamp. PACT key: exemplar.root.build_pipeline_result.

inputs.name: assessments
inputs.description: List of Assessment objects from circuit stage.

inputs.name: events
inputs.description: All ChroniclerEvents accumulated during pipeline execution.

error_cases.name: unknown_decision
error_cases.condition: report.decision is not a recognized ReviewDecision variant

preconditions: All inputs are valid and non-None
report.decision is a valid ReviewDecision
postconditions: Returned PipelineResult.exit_code is 0 for pass, 1 for warn, 2 for block
Returned PipelineResult.review_request is the input review_request
Returned PipelineResult.assessments is the input assessments list
Returned PipelineResult.report is the input report
Returned PipelineResult.events is the input events list
Returned PipelineResult.formatted_output is the input formatted_output string
Returned PipelineResult.output_format matches the input output_format

functions.name: _emit_event
functions.description: Internal fire-and-forget helper: constructs a ChroniclerEvent and emits it via the injected ChroniclerEmitter. Catches all exceptions from the emitter — failures are logged via stdlib logging but never propagate. Uses the injected clock for timestamp and id_generator for event_id. PACT key is passed as a payload field.

inputs.name: pact_key
inputs.description: PACT attribution key to embed in event payload.

inputs.name: accumulated_events
inputs.description: If provided, the constructed event is also appended to this list for inclusion in PipelineResult.

postconditions: If emitter succeeds, event was durably emitted to chronicle log
If emitter fails, failure is logged via stdlib logging — no exception propagates
If accumulated_events is provided, event is appended to it regardless of emitter success/failure
Event payload contains 'pact_key' field with the provided PACT key

structured_side_effects.description: Logs emitter failures via stdlib logging

functions.name: create_root
functions.description: Factory function that creates a fully configured Root instance. Loads ExemplarConfig via config.load_config (using optional explicit path), constructs Root with all defaults, and returns it. This is the primary entry point for both CLI and MCP server. PACT key: exemplar.root.create_root.

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Optional explicit path to YAML config file. If empty, config resolution follows the standard cascade: env var → conventional → builtin defaults.

error_cases.name: config_file_not_found
error_cases.condition: Explicit config_path does not exist on filesystem

error_cases.name: config_parse_error
error_cases.condition: Config file has invalid YAML syntax

error_cases.name: config_validation_error
error_cases.condition: Config file content fails Pydantic validation

error_cases.name: root_construction_failure
error_cases.condition: Root.__init__ raises ExemplarError (e.g., invalid circuit config)

preconditions: If config_path is provided and non-empty, it points to a readable YAML file
postconditions: Returned object is a fully constructed Root instance
Root._is_open is False (caller must use async with to open)
Config was loaded and validated successfully

structured_side_effects.description: Reads and parses YAML config file via config.load_config if path is provided or conventional path exists
Logs config resolution source and any warnings

functions.name: map_decision_to_exit_code
functions.description: Pure mapping function from ReviewDecision string to integer exit code. pass→0, warn→1, block→2. Used by _build_pipeline_result. PACT key: exemplar.root.build_pipeline_result.

validators.error_message: decision must be one of: pass, warn, block

error_cases.name: invalid_decision
error_cases.condition: decision is not 'pass', 'warn', or 'block'

preconditions: decision is a valid ReviewDecision variant string
postconditions: Returns 0 for 'pass', 1 for 'warn', 2 for 'block'
Return value is always in {0, 1, 2}

functions.name: get_config
functions.description: Read-only accessor returning the ExemplarConfig used by this Root instance. Useful for CLI/MCP introspection and debugging. PACT key: exemplar.root.get_config.
preconditions: Root was successfully constructed
postconditions: Returned value is the ExemplarConfig passed to __init__ (or loaded by create_root)
Returned config is a frozen Pydantic model — caller cannot mutate it

functions.name: get_circuit_router
functions.description: Read-only accessor returning the BatonCircuitRouter instance constructed by Root. Useful for CLI/MCP introspection and testing. PACT key: exemplar.root.get_circuit_router.
preconditions: Root was successfully constructed
postconditions: Returned value is the BatonCircuitRouter instance
Router's execution plan is accessible via get_execution_plan()

invariants: Root contains zero business logic — only wiring, sequencing, error handling at stage boundaries, and Chronicler event emission. All domain logic lives in sub-components.
Root is NOT a frozen Pydantic model — it holds mutable runtime state (component references, _is_open flag). All data flowing through the pipeline uses frozen schemas models.
All Chronicler event emissions from Root are fire-and-forget: emitter failures are caught, logged, and never propagate to the pipeline caller.
PACT key constants (exemplar.root.*) are embedded in every ChroniclerEvent payload emitted by Root and in every ExemplarError raised by Root.

Pipeline stage error classification is deterministic: intake failures with zero hunks are fatal; reviewer timeouts produce partial assessments (recoverable via circuit); seal/chronicler failures are ignorable (fire-and-forget).
Root.__init__ never performs file I/O. All file I/O initialization happens in __aenter__.
Root.__aexit__ never suppresses exceptions — it always returns False from the async context manager.
The injected ClockProvider is used for all timestamps in Root-emitted ChroniclerEvents and PipelineResult, enabling deterministic testing.
The injected id_generator is used for all event_id and report_id generation in Root, enabling deterministic testing.
create_root is the canonical entry point — CLI main() and MCP create_server() should both use it to obtain a Root instance.
Exit code mapping is deterministic and fixed: pass→0, warn→1, block→2, error→3.
Learner trust adjustment retrieval is fire-and-forget: if learner state is unavailable, default trust weights from ExemplarConfig are used without error.
Root holds exactly one BatonCircuitRouter instance, constructed once in __init__, reused across all run_pipeline calls.
If seal_report fails, the pipeline still returns a valid PipelineResult with an unsealed ReviewReport — sealing is never fatal.

data_access.rationale: Root orchestrates the full review pipeline which processes diff text that may contain secrets (classified as SECRET by Ledger), PII patterns (email addresses, names in comments), and internal API references. Root reads the raw diff (PUBLIC input), passes it through intake classification which may flag PII/SECRET content in hunks, then routes classified hunks through reviewers. Root writes INTERNAL-tier data: Chronicler event logs (audit trail with review request IDs, stage timings, PACT keys), Tessera seal chain entries (hash-chain proofs over report content), and pipeline result metadata. Root never directly reads or writes SECRET-tier data — Ledger classification labels flow through as metadata on DiffHunks, and the actual secret content is handled by intake and reviewers, not Root.

side_effects.rationale: Appends structured ChroniclerEvent JSON lines to the chronicle log file at each pipeline stage boundary for audit trail and governance compliance.
Appends TesseraSeal entries to the seal chain store file via SealChainStoreProtocol to maintain hash-chain integrity proofs over ReviewReports.
Reads learner state from .exemplar/learner/state.json to determine if trust adjustments should be applied (canary/primary phase) and to retrieve computed trust weights.
Initializes learner state directory and files during __aenter__ if they do not exist.
Reads the most recent chain_hash from seal chain store to link new seals to the existing chain.

authority.rationale: Root is the single authoritative owner of the end-to-end pipeline execution sequence (intake → circuit → assess → format → seal) and the pipeline lifecycle (resource initialization via async context manager, event accumulation, exit code mapping). No other component defines or modifies the pipeline stage ordering, error boundary classification, or PipelineResult assembly. Sub-components own their respective domains (intake owns diff parsing, assessor owns merge logic, etc.) but Root alone owns the orchestration wiring and lifecycle.
